message.content: System Binary Proxy Execution: CMSTP involves adversaries exploiting the Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer (CMSTP) to execute malicious code. CMSTP is a legitimate command-line tool used to install Connection Manager service profiles via INF files. Attackers can craft malicious INF files to execute harmful commands, load DLLs, or run COM scriptlets from remote servers. This technique can bypass security measures like AppLocker and User Account Control (UAC) because CMSTP is a trusted, signed binary. Notable groups such as Cobalt Group and MuddyWater have leveraged CMSTP to bypass defenses and deploy payloads like POWERSTATS. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting the use of CMSTP, implementing strict application whitelisting policies, and employing behavioral analysis to detect abnormal usage patterns.
